mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 3.94% represents a restrictive monetary stance. Sustained FEDFUNDS above 3.5% signals continued credit tightening that depresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS > 3.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.9% is near cyclical lows, indicating tight labor markets and wage pressure. A rise in UNRATE above 5.2% within the next 60 days signals deteriorating labor demand and recession risk, triggering equity selloffs as forward earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.2% within 60 calendar days
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.2%, or correlation between UNRATE change and 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts at 1006K units represent a sharp deceleration from 2006 peaks. Declining HOUST signals weakening residential investment and consumer wealth effects. A 15% month-over-month drop in HOUST triggers negative equity returns within 20 trading days as forward GDP growth expectations fall.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the magnitude of negative returns following HOUST drops does not exceed baseline volatility-adjusted returns by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 114.23 (2017=100) reflects manufacturing capacity utilization. A sustained 3-month decline in INDPRO below 113.5 signals supply-side weakness and deflationary pressure, reducing corporate margins and equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 113.5 and remains there for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO meets trigger condition, or Rank IC between INDPRO and 60-day returns is below 0.02 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales at 382,906M and payroll employment at 138,102K show consumer spending resilience. However, when RSAFS growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month AND PAYEMS growth falls below 0.1% month-over-month simultaneously, this joint signal indicates demand destruction. The interaction triggers negative 20-day SPX returns as forward consumption expectations collapse.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth < 0.2% AND PAYEMS MoM growth < 0.1% in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 382906M and PAYEMS at 138102K as of 2008-01-15
regime_hint: demand shock with synchronized labor and consumption weakness
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where both conditions are met, or the joint signal produces lower predictive power (Rank IC) than either signal alone over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI at 211.68 (1982-1984=100) reflects moderate inflation. When CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains below 4.0%, the Fed is behind the inflation curve. This regime-conditional signal predicts negative equity returns over 5 trading days as market reprices inflation risk and expects future rate hikes.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 211.68 and FEDFUNDS at 3.94% as of 2008-01-15
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) does not outperform either individual signal in terms of Rank IC over any 6-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 138,102K is near cyclical peak. A 3-month cumulative decline in PAYEMS of more than 0.5% signals labor market deterioration and recession onset. This regime-conditional signal in a late-cycle expansion predicts negative 60-day SPX returns as forward earnings and GDP growth expectations fall sharply.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines cumulatively by more than 0.5% over 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where PAYEMS meets trigger condition, or the signal fails to produce statistically significant negative returns (t-test p > 0.10) relative to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP at 11,677.4B (chained 2017 dollars) is the broadest demand measure. When GDPC1 growth decelerates to below 2.0% annualized (measured quarterly), combined with HOUST falling below 950K units, this dual-domain signal indicates demand exhaustion. The interaction predicts negative 60-day SPX returns as recession probability rises.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND HOUST < 950K in same quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11677.4B and HOUST at 1006K as of 2008-01-15
regime_hint: demand-side recession risk with housing collapse
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where both conditions are met, or the joint signal's Rank IC is not significantly higher than the individual GDPC1 or HOUST signals alone over any 12-month rolling window.
